title: Heat Pump — No Heating in Winter
complaint: Heat pump runs in winter but blows cold air. Auxiliary heat works.
initialData: Indoor air feels cold from supply registers
Auxiliary heat strips work when Emergency Heat selected

s1.prompt: The compressor runs but blows cold air in heat mode. Aux heat works. What is the most likely cause?

options.label: Reversing valve stuck in cooling position or O-terminal wiring issue

s1-wrong.prompt: The compressor is running (audible). A dirty filter would not cause cold air in heat mode. The system is running in cooling mode when it should be heating.

options.label: Check the reversing valve and O-terminal

s1-partial.prompt: Low charge is possible but would not cause the system to blow cold air — it would just reduce heating capacity. The system appears to be in cooling mode.

options.label: Check the reversing valve and O-terminal

s2.prompt: You check the thermostat O-terminal. In heat mode, O should be de-energized (O-type system). You measure 24V at O in heat mode. What does this mean?

options.label: The thermostat is energizing O in heat mode — it should not be
The reversing valve solenoid is failed open

s2-wrong.prompt: On O-type systems, O is energized in COOLING and de-energized in HEATING. 24V at O in heat mode means the thermostat is commanding cooling.

options.label: The thermostat is misconfigured or failed

s2-partial.prompt: Possible, but first confirm the thermostat is not sending the wrong signal. 24V at O in heat mode points to the thermostat.

s3.prompt: You check the thermostat settings. The O/B reversing valve setting is configured as "B" (energize in heating) instead of "O" (energize in cooling). What do you do?

options.label: Change the O/B setting to "O" and verify heating operation

s3-wrong.prompt: The thermostat is misconfigured, not failed. Changing the O/B setting is the correct fix — no parts needed.

options.label: Change O/B setting to "O" and verify

pass.prompt: Correct. The thermostat O/B setting was configured for a B-type system (Rheem/Ruud) instead of O-type. Correcting the setting restored normal heating.
pass.feedback: O-type: reversing valve energized in cooling. B-type: energized in heating. Always verify thermostat configuration matches the equipment.
